scenario: Cygnus Bank, a digital bank, serves 40000 customers on Camunda 8.8. A developer is wiring a Radio field whose options come from a FEEL expression, and the team has chosen the shortest shorthand by supplying only a value per entry, with no label, so they must respect the documented constraint on which types the value parameter may take in that no-label case.
question: Which rule governs the supported types for the value parameter when no label is provided for the entry?

options.text: The value parameter must be a string and integer or boolean entries are rejected when no label has been provided for the entry
The value parameter is restricted to types string, number, or boolean when no label is provided for that option entry
The value parameter may be any non-null type as long as a string fallback is also supplied at the form schema level configuration
The value parameter must be a string when no label is provided and any numeric or boolean entry is dropped at form load time

a.text: Incorrect. The docs allow number and boolean for the value parameter when no label is provided; only types beyond string, number, and boolean are excluded in that no-label case.

b.text: Correct. The docs state that when a label is not provided, the value parameter is restricted to the types string, number, and boolean; richer non-null types require an explicit label.

c.text: Incorrect. The any non-null rule for value only applies when a label is provided; without a label the type set is restricted to string, number, and boolean by the docs.

d.text: Incorrect. Number and boolean values are valid for entries without a label; only types outside of string, number, and boolean are excluded by the docs in that no-label case.

explanation: When the label parameter is provided, the value parameter can be any non-null type. When no label is given, the value parameter is restricted to the basic types string, number, and boolean so the runtime can still render a sensible label from the value itself.
